seoTitle: Connect American Express to QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Link your American Express business card to QuickBooks Online for automatic transaction downloads and easier expense tracking.
intro: Reconciling Amex statements by hand eats into time you could spend elsewhere. This guide shows how to securely connect your American Express business card to QuickBooks Online so transactions flow in automatically for categorization.

steps.heading: Open Bank Transactions
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Bank Transactions, to reach your connected accounts dashboard.

steps.heading: Search for American Express
steps.body: Click Link Account, then type American Express or Amex into the financial institution search bar.

steps.heading: Authorize the connection
steps.body: Select the official American Express link and log in with your corporate credentials on the secure authorization portal.

steps.heading: Select your business cards
steps.body: Uncheck any personal cards and choose only the business Amex accounts you want to sync.

steps.heading: Map and connect
steps.body: Link the account to a Credit Card liability line in your Chart of Accounts, set a historical data window, then click Connect.

faq.question: Can I sync only my business cards and not personal ones?
faq.answer: Yes, during setup you can uncheck personal profiles and select only your target business Amex accounts.

faq.question: How far back can I pull historical transactions?
faq.answer: You can choose your historical data fetch window from the calendar dropdown during setup to establish earlier balance records.

faq.question: What account type should Amex be mapped to?
faq.answer: Map it to a Credit Card liability line in your Chart of Accounts so balances track correctly.

faq.question: How do I categorize transactions after connecting?
faq.answer: Once connected, review your live bank feed grid and use the matching or categorization tools to process incoming rows.
